finding: Silica drift from Block N-14 is reducing DR readiness and compression strength margin.
whatHappened: Projected compression strength falls below threshold in 2.5 hours.
why: N-14 feed at 38% has higher silica and variable moisture; balling disc speed is not compensating.
action: Reduce N-14 feed to 22%, blend E-07 at 38%, increase binder by 0.04%, adjust disc speed by 3%, raise induration Zone 3 by 12°C.
ifIgnored: Off-spec risk may rise above 31%, reducing saleable DR pellet tons.
expectedImpact: Off-spec risk drops from 31% to 7%; production recovery improves to 96% of plan.

finding: CV-204 bearing stress indicates high failure probability within 18 hours.
whatHappened: Bearing temperature is 18% above baseline and vibration is above threshold.
why: Load variability from crusher surge and misalignment signature on the conveyor drive-end bearing.
action: Create 45-minute work order during feed transition and reserve bearing kit BRG-CV204-XL.
ifIgnored: Unplanned conveyor stop could constrain the concentrator and pellet plant for 6-10 hours.
expectedImpact: Reduces failure probability from 72% to 19% and protects $0.9M of throughput exposure.

finding: Peak tariff exposure in the next 90 minutes can be avoided without production loss.
whatHappened: Grinding load overlaps with the 14:00-16:00 high-price window.
why: Current power demand is 82.4 MW and non-critical mill load can be deferred 45 minutes.
action: Shift non-critical grinding load by 45 minutes and maintain furnace profile.
ifIgnored: Cost per ton rises and carbon intensity remains elevated through the shift.
expectedImpact: Saves $42K and improves carbon intensity by 3.4%.

finding: High wind creates dust risk at Zone S-3 stockpile.
whatHappened: Wind gusts above 35 mph combined with stockpile activity are pushing dust index near limit.
why: Exposed stockpile faces and truck movement are aligned with gust direction.
action: Activate water suppression, restrict vehicle movement, and schedule dust inspection round.
ifIgnored: Dust index may exceed permit threshold and create worker exposure risk.
expectedImpact: Dust index reduces from 78 to 59 within 40 minutes.

finding: DR-grade shipment to Customer A faces a 9-hour railcar availability delay.
whatHappened: P2 stockpile meets quality threshold, but allocated railcars are short by 28.
why: Railcar turnback from previous cycle is delayed and loadout queue prioritizes P4.
action: Resequence P2 loadout before P4 and rebalance 28 railcars from lower-margin order.
ifIgnored: Demurrage risk and customer service penalty exposure increase.
expectedImpact: Protects $0.38M and improves on-time dispatch probability to 93%.
